title: schradera subandina
intro: Schradera subandina is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, leveraging its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Schradera subandina include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as wounds and infections. Schradera subandina is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations may also involve the use of its leaves, bark, or roots to create teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
